channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Notational Intelligence, Linus Lee | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: Linus Lee discusses notational intelligence as how the ways we write ideas down shape our thinking. He explores what makes good notation including abstraction and suggestiveness, then proposes that deep learning models might invent entirely new notations and serve as simulators for conceptual frameworks.

item.title: Agency in Language, Alane Suhr | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: Alane Suhr, Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley, discusses agency in language and what language models fundamentally are. She argues that humans, not the concept of AI itself, hold the agency in how we use and interpret language models.

item.title: Intelligence Efficiency, Ben Geist | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: Ben Geist, Research Engineer at Ramp, argues that intelligence efficiency improves through better context and entropy reduction rather than simply scaling compute. He discusses token spend patterns and contrasts computational work with contextual understanding in making models more efficient.

item.title: The Memory Problem, Baseten | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: Baseten's research team presents an approach to compress the key-value cache for long-horizon agents, enabling near-lossless retrieval of relevant context during inference. The method uses iterative compaction and relates compressed caches to multilayer perceptrons.

item.title: Explaining Culture to Technology, Paul Ford | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: Paul Ford discusses what technologists can learn from magazine production and proposes that culture is fundamentally a distributed, lossy prediction model rather than merely its output. He explores connections between culture, consciousness, and rhetoric, framing culture as an operating system for society.

item.title: Why We're Not Too Late to Stop AI Hackers | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
item.description: EO: Damien Lewke argues that AI has dramatically lowered the barrier to launching cyberattacks, enabling two people with a GPU to execute what once required elite teams and years of preparation. He advocates for an "assume breach" defensive posture and identifies three behavioral signals that reveal attackers already inside networks.

item.title: AI Agents: How Do We Take Back Control? | Trailer
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: A documentary hosted by Alex Kantrowitz examines the gap between AI agent deployment and organizational control, featuring interviews with technologists, executives, and government officials. Research of 750 leaders found that 80% of organizations deploy AI agents but only 10% feel in control, with only one-third of production agents running with zero human oversight.

item.title: Slash AI Costs: Smart Model Choice for Max ROAI! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof advises matching model complexity to task requirements rather than defaulting to high-capability models. The strategy aims to maximize return on AI investment through cost-effective model selection.

item.title: AI Ecosystems: Claude vs. SAP/Salesforce & Living Artifacts Explained! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof discusses trade-offs in AI ecosystems, comparing expensive AI queries against self-updating living artifacts. The video examines cost-effectiveness considerations for integrating AI into dynamic systems like those provided by SAP and Salesforce.

item.title: Every Claude Tool Explained: Master Full Ecosystem in 20 Minutes
item.description: AI Master: A comprehensive tutorial covering Claude's integrated ecosystem, which includes Chat, Code, Cowork, Design, and Routines products. The video explains how these tools work together for workflows spanning research, coding, collaboration, automation, and content creation, positioning Claude as an operating system for work rather than just a chatbot.

item.title: Unlock Free SaaS with Claude Artifacts: Your AI Power-Up! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof presents Claude Artifacts as free SaaS functionality for sales, research, and productivity tasks. The video highlights how to use Claude's artifact feature as a cost-free alternative to paid software tools.

item.title: GLM-5.2 + Z-Code (Ultra Mode - Free Tier): FABLE LEVEL PERFORMANCE!
item.description: AICodeKing: A tutorial demonstrating GLM-5.2 integrated with Z-Code for coding tasks, using KingMode, planning skills, verification workflows, and MCPs. The setup aims to achieve frontier-model-level coding performance on free tier by combining structured planning, agent-style skills, and systematic verification processes.

item.title: AI Prices Soar: Fable's Fall & China's Rise? #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Frontier AI models are becoming too expensive and vulnerable. Chinese open-source alternatives are emerging as cheaper options, potentially reshaping the global AI landscape amid rising geopolitical tensions.

item.title: US Over-regulation Gives China AI Advantage! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof argues that US over-regulation of AI, following EU precedent, undermines American competitiveness and allows China to advance its global AI dominance. The claim is that regulatory caution in the US creates a structural disadvantage relative to less-constrained competitors.

item.title: AI's REAL Strength: Greenfield Projects, Not Legacy Code! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: AI is effective at building greenfield projects from scratch but faces significant challenges integrating into legacy systems. Poor documentation and system complexity create obstacles that hinder AI performance even in sophisticated systems.

item.title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
item.description: AI Engineer: RL Nabors argues that small local models can handle most inference workloads more cost-effectively than frontier models, and provides a framework for deciding when to replace frontier calls with smaller models. The talk covers evaluation vocabulary and methods for measuring model performance on production traces to make data-driven model selection decisions.

item.title: The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents
item.description: AI Engineer: Justin Schroeder argues that domain-specific agents composed together form a superior architecture compared to monolithic skills. He contends that this composition-based approach enables smaller models to perform real work, improves reliability and cost efficiency, and moves AI from internal tools to customer-facing products.

item.title: You Can't Prompt the Room: The Last Skill AI Won't Replace - Balázs Horváth, VisualLabs
item.description: AI Engineer: Balázs Horváth argues that AI has commoditized code generation, making requirements elicitation and stakeholder alignment the new bottleneck in software development. He presents three practical tools for this work: story mapping, a four-question value framework, and the VAD thinking path, using a VisualLabs hackathon case study where 17 of 21 agent ideas were abandoned due to lack of business value, not technology limitations.

item.title: AI Costs Skyrocketing? UBS Report Reveals Smarter Spending Secrets! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A UBS report highlights that companies are strategically optimizing AI budgets through model selection and cost-effective solutions. The video suggests that cost optimization and solutions from China are shaping the future of AI investment as AI costs rise.

item.title: Deterministic Infra for Non-Deterministic AI Agents - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
item.description: AI Engineer: Nishant Gupta from Meta discusses infrastructure design for autonomous AI agents that operate reliably in production environments. The talk addresses the gap between deterministic microservice infrastructure and non-deterministic LLM-based agents, covering orchestration, observability, retry handling, safety guardrails, and failure recovery patterns.

item.title: Anthropic Just Confirmed It: The 2028 AI Warning Is Real
item.description: AI Revolution: The video presents claims that Anthropic executives have outlined a 2028 timeline for recursive self-improvement in AI, citing statements from Jack Clark about Claude versions, MirrorCode's autonomous software development, and GPT-5.6 Sol's capabilities. It frames AI-assisted AI development as a major inflection point in the competitive landscape.

item.title: The Agentic AI Engineer - Benedikt Sanftl, Mutagent
item.description: AI Engineer: The Agentic AI Engineer is a multi-agent system steered by an orchestrator that implements an eval-driven development loop for building AI agents. The system combines specification, building, evaluation, diagnosis, monitoring, and optimization of AI agents. Benedikt Sanftl from Mutagent presented this concept with a live agent demo.

item.title: The Prompt is the Platform - Dominik Tornow, Resonate HQ
item.description: AI Engineer: Dominik Tornow argues that coding agents challenge traditional software engineering by synthesizing bespoke systems on demand rather than relying on general-purpose libraries and frameworks. He discusses where agents succeed and fail, and proposes making the prompt the core platform and specification the product.

item.title: Using RL Agent to Detect and Remediate ETL Pipeline Failures - Anna Marie Benzon
item.description: AI Engineer: An RL-guided agent automates ETL pipeline failure recovery by detecting anomalies such as schema drift and data-quality issues, then selecting repair actions like retry or rollback. The system combines deterministic rules, Q-learning, and safety guardrails to achieve minutes-scale recovery.

item.title: Your Agent Failed in Prod. Good Luck Reproducing It. - Tisha Chawla & Susheem Koul, Microsoft
item.description: AI Engineer: The record and replay pattern captures every model invocation, tool execution, memory access, and state transition into an append-only event log to enable deterministic replay of failed agent executions in production. This approach moves beyond state recovery and API mocking to provide engineers with the ability to step through execution traces line by line for root cause analysis. The pattern brings low-level systems debugging philosophy (similar to Mozilla rr) into autonomous agent loops to ad

item.title: AI is COSTING Companies a FORTUNE! 💰 Shocking Truth! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: AI API costs are rising and pressuring company margins. Future price increases will force businesses to reassess their AI strategies, with profitability emerging as a key challenge for the sector.

item.title: Voice In, Visuals Out: The Agony and the Ecstasy - Allen Pike, Forestwalk Labs
item.description: AI Engineer: Allen Pike discusses voice-in, visuals-out as a paradigm for LLM-powered products, arguing it represents optimal AI user experience. The talk covers techniques for achieving low-latency responsive AI agents and design lessons from building such products at Forestwalk Labs.

item.title: AI-Driven Multi-Document Correlation for Financial Compliance - Varsha Shah, Independent
item.description: AI Engineer: A framework combines graph-based entity correlation, adaptive probabilistic risk modeling, and cross-jurisdictional normalization to detect fraud patterns across multiple enterprise documents. Evaluation on three million anonymized records across four jurisdictions demonstrates improved fraud detection accuracy, reduced false positives, and lower manual audit effort.

item.title: We Cut 94% of AI Coding Tokens With a Local Code Index - Rajkumar Sakthivel, Tesco
item.description: AI Engineer: Rajkumar Sakthivel describes a local code indexing approach that reduces token usage by 94% in AI coding agents. The system uses AST-aware chunking, combined vector and keyword search, and lightweight relationship tracking to send only relevant context instead of entire files. The technique achieves faster responses, lower latency, and improved accuracy while keeping all data local.

item.title: CIOs: Slash Software Costs by Rebuilding with AI! ROI Matters! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: CIOs should audit software spending and identify systems that can be rebuilt with AI to achieve major cost reductions. The pitch emphasizes calculating AI's ROI and optimizing technology investments as a competitive advantage in enterprise settings.

item.title: Your Agent Is Wasting Tokens and You Don't Know It - Erik Hanchett, AWS
item.description: AI Engineer: Erik Hanchett discusses three optimization techniques for reducing agent token waste in production: prompt caching, model routing, and improved tool loop efficiency. These lightweight changes cut costs without sacrificing quality or requiring prompt rewrites.

item.title: OpenClaw in Your Hand: Building a Physical AI Terminal - Lech Kalinowski, Callstack
item.description: AI Engineer: Vault is a handheld AI terminal built on an ESP32-S3 with dual displays (OLED and e-paper) that runs local language models and autonomous agents. The system uses NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM to serve a 120B parameter model locally, enabling shell, assist, control, and game modes without cloud dependencies. The architecture separates firmware from a Python backend that dispatches to local models and agents, displaying real-time reasoning on e-paper.

item.title: Bypassing the Multimodal Tax: Hybrid RAG, SQL RRF & UI Telemetry - Abed Matini, Ogilvy
item.description: AI Engineer: This talk covers production-scale architectural patterns for RAG systems including local-first document ingestion with OCR pipelines, database-first retrieval combining dense embeddings with sparse keyword indices via Reciprocal Rank Fusion, and client-side telemetry with prompt-injection validation. The approach emphasizes cost optimization by minimizing cloud API token usage through local processing.

item.title: AI System Design: From Idea to Production - Apoorva Joshi, MongoDB
item.description: AI Engineer: Apoorva Joshi presents a structured framework for designing AI systems from idea to production, arguing that identifying the business problem, defining success metrics, and selecting appropriate architecture are more critical than coding ability. The talk addresses why most AI systems fail to reach production due to poor early-stage decisions and demonstrates the framework applied to real-world problems.

item.title: Is Chat AI Killing All Your SaaS Subscriptions? #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof questions whether a single AI chatbot could consolidate functionality currently spread across multiple specialized SaaS applications. The commentary explores whether conversational AI and changes to user interfaces may reshape the SaaS landscape.

item.title: Build a full stack app with Antigravity voice prompts & sub-agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Antigravity is a platform that uses voice prompts to orchestrate teams of AI sub-agents for full-stack application development. The demonstrated workflow builds a multilingual note-taking app with Vite frontend, Go and SQLite backend, and Docker Compose orchestration.

item.title: Open vs Closed AI: The Future Is CHEAPER & SMARTER #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof argues the performance gap between open-source and closed-source AI models is narrowing rapidly. The claim is that open models will prove cheaper and smarter, with implications for business adoption and developer choice.

item.title: Claude Just Changed How Enterprises Work Forever (Claude Tag)
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof discusses Claude Tag, a new feature from Anthropic that impacts enterprise workflows. The video covers Claude Tag, industry challenges including SaaS market conditions, and open source developments in China.

item.title: AI’s True Cyber Risk: Fable, Mythos, China, and The Rest — With Alex Stamos
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Alex Stamos argues that the government-mandated recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models does not address a genuine cyber threat and reflects industry dynamics rather than substantive security concerns. He contends that the real cybersecurity breakthrough occurred with earlier models like Opus 4 and GPT-5, and warns that the recall decision undermines America's competitive position against China.

item.title: How to Build A Self-Improving System with Claude Code
item.description: Austin Marchese: Austin Marchese presents a 5-step B.U.I.L.D. Framework for building self-improving systems with Claude Code. The framework covers creating a base framework, ingesting data, setting up pipelines, implementing improvement loops, and avoiding over-engineering. The approach emphasizes automating data pipelines and iterative system refinement.

item.title: Google FULLY FREE Vibe-gravity (VIBE CODE 2.0): FREE & BEST!
item.description: AICodeKing: Google AI Studio added Android app generation directly in the browser, supporting Kotlin and Jetpack Compose project creation with cloud emulator preview and Web USB deployment. A new Design Variations feature generates and applies different UI layouts visually without complex prompts.

item.title: GPT 5.6 explained in 8min..
item.description: Caleb Writes Code: Caleb Writes Code discusses OpenAI's announcement of GPT 5.6 and its regulatory status as the AI industry matures. The video compares models from Anthropic and notes that GPT 5.6 faces similar scrutiny. OpenAI is consolidating its model lineup to three variants: Sol, Terra, and Luna.

item.title: GPT 5.6, Mythos ban lifted, realtime avatars, Seedance 2.5, brain ultrasound: AI NEWS
item.description: AI Search: AI Search presents a video news roundup covering major AI releases including GPT 5.6, Seedance 2.5, Seed 2.1, Krea 2, and hardware announcements from OpenAI, IBM, and others. The video also reports on the lifting of a ban on Mythos and discusses emerging applications in brain ultrasound imaging and real-time avatars.

item.title: GPT 5.6 Sol Just Blew Up The AI World
item.description: AI Revolution: OpenAI launched GPT 5.6 Sol with limited access following U.S. government pressure, emphasizing new coding and cyber capabilities. The company also introduced Jalapeño, a custom inference chip developed with Broadcom, signaling efforts to reduce Nvidia dependence and control the full AI stack.

item.title: What Machiavelli thought freedom actually was - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer on Machiavelli's conception of freedom. The discussion explores how Machiavelli understood liberty in contrast to modern interpretations.

item.title: Building a dynamic personal site with Antigravity and Gemma 4
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Rody Davis demonstrates using Google Antigravity with Gemma to build a serverless, VS Code-styled website generator. The site features theme switching and offline vector search, using Gemma for post summarization and embedding-gemma for compile-time vectorization to enable dynamic recommendations entirely offline.

item.title: Build a multi-agent system: A2A & Agent Registry
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud introduces Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol, described as "HTTP for AI agents," and Agent Registry for managing multi-agent systems at scale. The system enables agents to discover and communicate with each other dynamically without hardcoded URLs, addressing agent sprawl through agent cards, sync/async communication, and MCP server integration.

item.title: GPT 5.6 SOL: TBH, IT'S OKAY.. I have SERIOUS CONCERNS.
item.description: AICodeKing: AICodeKing reviews GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna models, discussing their limited availability, benchmark performance against prior versions, and pricing. The author concludes these represent scaled-up versions rather than revolutionary capability leaps, expressing concerns about specific unsafe behaviors and questioning their value proposition versus cheaper alternatives like GPT-5.5.

item.title: Automating workflows and file organization with Antigravity
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Antigravity is a tool for automating file system workflows and organization across multiple projects. It uses time boxes and tags to automatically manage files and repositories, reducing manual effort in searching and organizing code assets.

item.title: GPT 5.6 banned, Fable banned… it’s actually over.
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej reports on bans affecting GPT 5.6 and Fable, suggesting a significant shift in the AI landscape. The framing "it's actually over" indicates a critical take on the state of competition or regulation in large language models.
